subject: Strategy and Factory Patterns in Practice

content.principles: 1. Strategy: Encapsulates interchangeable algorithms. Used to eliminate complex if-else/switch blocks. 2. Factory: Encapsulates object creation. Decouples the caller from the concrete classes. 3. Combination: Use Factory to produce the correct Strategy instance based on a type/identifier, then execute the strategy.
pitfalls: Over-engineering: Creating too many classes for trivial logic.
Static Factory preventing easy unit testing/mocking (prefer dependency injection).
Strategy leaking internal state to the context/caller.
